volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Epidicus (Section 5.2.679 - Section 5.2.723b)

sections.label: Section 5.2.679
sections.original: Dum sine me quaeras , quaeras mea causa vel medio in mari .
sections.translation: So long as you look for him without me , you may look for him even in the middle of the sea , as far as I ’ m concerned .

sections.label: Section 5.2.680
sections.original: Quid me quaeris ? quid laboras ? quid hunc sollicitas ? ecce me .
sections.translation: Why are you looking for me ? Why are you taking this trouble ? Why are you worrying him ? ( Pointing to APAECIDES .) See , here I am . Have I run away from you ? Have I betaken myself from the house ? Have I hid myself from your sight ? I don ’ t entreat you either . Do you wish to bind me ? Look then , I present my hands . ( Holding out his hands .) You have got thongs ; I saw you purchase them . Why do you delay now ? Do bind me !

sections.label: Section 5.2.685
sections.original: Ilicet , vadimonium ultro mi hic facit .
sections.translation: It ’ s all over [*] . He gives bail to me of his own accord for his appearance .

sections.label: Section 5.2.685b
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you bind me ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.686
sections.translation: A wicked slave , upon my faith !

sections.label: Section 5.2.686b
sections.translation: Really , Apaecides , I don ’ t want you to intercede for me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.687b
sections.translation: You easily obtain your wish , Epidicus .

sections.label: Section 5.2.688
sections.translation: Are you going to do anything ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.688b
sections.translation: What , at your pleasure ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.688c
sections.original: Meo hercle vero atque hau tuo
sections.translation: Troth , at my own , indeed , and not at yours , are these hands to be bound by you to-day .

sections.label: Section 5.2.689b
sections.translation: But I don ’ t choose ; I shan ’ t bind them .

sections.label: Section 5.2.690
sections.original: Tragulam in te inicere adornat , nescio quam fabricam facit .
sections.translation: He ’ s preparing to throw a dragnet over you ; he ’ s framing some plan , I know not what .

sections.label: Section 5.2.691
sections.original: Tibi moram facis , cum égo solutus asto . age , inquam , colliga .
sections.translation: You are causing delay to yourself , while I stand unbound ; bind them , I tell you , bind them together .

sections.label: Section 5.2.692
sections.original: At mihi magis lubet solutum te rogitare .
sections.translation: But I choose rather to question you unbound .

sections.label: Section 5.2.692b
sections.translation: But you shall know nothing .

sections.label: Section 5.2.693
sections.translation: What am I to do ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.693b
sections.translation: What are you to do ? Let him be complied with .

sections.label: Section 5.2.693c
sections.translation: You are a worthy person , Apaecides .

sections.label: Section 5.2.694
sections.translation: Hold out your hands then .

sections.label: Section 5.2.694b
sections.translation: They don ’ t at all object ; bind them fast together too .

sections.label: Section 5.2.695
sections.translation: Don ’ t be afraid .

sections.label: Section 5.2.695b
sections.translation: I ’ m submissive .

sections.label: Section 5.2.695c
sections.translation: When the job is done , then pronounce your judgment [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.2.696
sections.original: Bene hoc habet . age nunciam ex me exquire , rogita quod lubet .
sections.translation: Now then , to the point , with your enquiries of me ; ask me what you please .

sections.label: Section 5.2.697
sections.original: Qua fiducia primum , quae emptast nudiustertius ,
sections.translation: In the first place , with what assurance did you presume to say that she who was bought the day before yesterday was my daughter ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.698b
sections.translation: I chose to ; on that assurance .

sections.label: Section 5.2.699
sections.translation: Do you say so ? You chose to ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.699b
sections.original: Aio . vel da pignus , ni ea sit filia .
sections.translation: I do say so . Even lay me a wager that she is not the daughter —

sections.label: Section 5.2.700
sections.translation: A woman whom the mother declares she doesn ’ t know ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.700b
sections.translation: Then , if she isn ’ t the daughter of her mother , make me a bet about it , my didrachm to your talent [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.2.701b
sections.translation: Why , that ’ s a mere catch . But who is this woman ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.702b
sections.original: Tui gnati amica , ut omnem rem scias .
sections.translation: The mistress of your son , that you may know the whole matter .

sections.label: Section 5.2.703
sections.original: Dedin tibi minas triginta ob filiam ?
sections.translation: Did I not give you the thirty minae for my daughter ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.703b
sections.translation: I confess that they were given , and that with that money I purchased this Music-girl , your son ’ s mistress ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.705
sections.original: pro tua filia : is te eam ob rem tetigi triginta minis .
sections.translation: instead of your daughter . As to those thirty minae , in fact , I imposed upon you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.706
sections.original: Quo modo mé ludos fecisti de illa conducticia
sections.translation: For what reason did you play a trick upon me about that hired Music-girl ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.707b
sections.original: Factum hercle vero , et recte factum iudico .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I really did do it , and I think it was rightly done .

sections.label: Section 5.2.708
sections.original: Quid postremo argento factum est quod dedi ?
sections.translation: In the last place , what has been done with the silver I gave you ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.708b
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you ; I gave it to a person neither bad nor yet good [*] — your son Stratippocles .

sections.label: Section 5.2.710
sections.translation: Why did you dare to give it him ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.710b
sections.translation: Because it pleased me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.710c
sections.translation: Plague on it ! what insolence is this ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.711
sections.translation: Am I to be bawled at just as if I were a slave ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.711b
sections.translation: As you are a free man , I rejoice .

sections.label: Section 5.2.712
sections.translation: I have deserved to become so .

sections.label: Section 5.2.712b
sections.translation: You — deserved ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.712c
sections.translation: Go look in-doors ; I ’ ll let you know that this is the truth .

sections.label: Section 5.2.713b
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.713c
sections.translation: The thing itself will tell you so at once . Only do go in-doors .

sections.label: Section 5.2.714b
sections.translation: Aye , aye ! this is not without its meaning .

sections.label: Section 5.2.714c
sections.translation: Do you keep an eye on him , Apaecides .

sections.label: Section 5.2.715
sections.translation: What ’ s the meaning of this , Epidicus ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.715b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , it ’ s with the greatest injustice that I ’ m standing here bound , through whose aid his daughter has been discovered to-day .

sections.label: Section 5.2.717
sections.original: Ain tu te illius invenisse filiam ?
sections.translation: Do you say that you have found his daughter ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.717b
sections.translation: I have found her , and she is at home . But how hard it is , when , for services done , you reap a harvest of evil .

sections.label: Section 5.2.719
sections.original: Quamne hodie per urbem uterque sumus defessi quaerere ?
sections.translation: What , her whom this day we ’ ve both been weary with seeking throughout the city ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.720
sections.original: Ego sum defessus reperire , vos defessi quaerere .
sections.translation: I ’ m weary with finding , you weary with seeking .

sections.label: Section 5.2.721
sections.original: Quid isti oratis opere tanto ? mi orandum esse intellego ,
sections.translation: Why do you entreat me thus earnestly in his behalf ? For I find that he does deserve that I should be pleased to act according to his deserts . ( To EPIDICUS .) Hold out your hands , you , that I may unbind them .

sections.label: Section 5.2.723
sections.translation: Don ’ t you touch them .

sections.label: Section 5.2.723b
sections.translation: But do you present them to me .
